meta.title: IGCSE Maths: Core, Extended & Additional Mathematics Online
meta.description: Live online IGCSE Maths course for Cambridge Core, Extended and Additional Mathematics: non-calculator fluency and past-paper technique on real mark schemes.
meta.level: IGCSE students, years 9-11: Core, Extended and Additional Maths
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + weekly past-paper work
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + documented paper-score progression

program_overview.title: IGCSE Mathematics Mastery
program_overview.tagline: Core, Extended or Additional, taught to the top grade, syllabus-exact.
program_overview.description: An 18-month live program for IGCSE mathematics, matched to the student's exact board and entry: Cambridge 0580/0980 at Core or Extended tier, Edexcel International GCSE, and Additional Mathematics 0606 for strong students. The tier decision caps or opens grades before a single answer is written, and Cambridge's updated syllabus put a non-calculator paper in front of every candidate, so this course trains bare-hands fluency deliberately, teaches the Extended reach early enough to settle, and converts understanding into marks through past-paper cycles against real mark schemes.
what_makes_this_different: Syllabus-exact: the mentor confirms your school's board and codes first, and teaches to them, 0580/0980, Edexcel 4MA1, or 0606
Non-calculator fluency trained in every class, because the newer papers examine it and calculator-raised students get caught
Tier-honest: evidence for the Core-vs-Extended decision, and a real runway from Core-risk to Extended-comfortable
Additional Maths 0606 taught as the serious second subject it is, the best A-Level preparation on the syllabus list
Mark-scheme literacy: method marks, follow-through and working discipline taught explicitly
Understanding-first teaching so mocks stop sliding as content deepens
One dedicated mentor across the whole IGCSE run
One full interactive hour per class, twice a week

learning_path.phase_1: The Core foundation built properly: number, algebra as a language, and calculator-free fluency as a habit
learning_path.phase_2: The Extended reach with settling time: functions, trigonometry, vectors and probability at full depth
learning_path.phase_3: Additional Maths 0606 for the strong (or Extended consolidation), then past-paper craft into the exam series

career_outcomes: An IGCSE maths grade that opens sixth-form and A-Level doors
Extended-tier command that makes A-Level maths a step, not a cliff
0606 students: genuine pre-A-Level maturity including introductory calculus
Exam craft, mark schemes, timing, working discipline, that transfers to every future exam

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: The Core Foundation, Built Properly (Months 1-6)
phase_1_foundation.description: Everything on the Extended papers stands on Core material owned deeply: number fluency without a calculator, algebra as a language rather than ritual, and the geometry and data basics. The diagnostic finds the real starting point; this phase makes it solid.

week_1_2.title: Diagnostic, Syllabus Code and Tier Fit
week_1_2.description: Full diagnostic: board and syllabus code confirmed, tier fit assessed honestly, non-calculator fluency and algebra base measured
topics: Board and syllabus code confirmed: 0580, 0980 or 4MA1
Core versus Extended tier fit assessed honestly
week_1_2.practice: A mixed non-calculator and algebra diagnostic set matched to the suspected tier.
week_1_2.assessment: Baseline grade estimate with a written Core-or-Extended recommendation and a topic-priority map.

week_3_4.title: Number Fluency Rebuilt
week_3_4.description: Number fluency rebuilt: fractions, decimals, percentages and estimation as a checking habit, the non-calculator paper's exact territory
topics: Percentage of, increase, decrease and reverse
Converting between fractions, decimals and percentages
Estimation and rounding as a check
week_3_4.practice: A non-calculator number set with estimation checks on every answer.

week_5_6.title: Ratio, Proportion and Standard Form
week_5_6.description: Ratio, proportion and rates of change; standard form with real scale
week_5_6.practice: Ratio, proportion and standard-form questions in applied contexts.

week_7_8.title: Indices, Surds, Bounds and Accuracy
week_7_8.description: Indices and surds from meaning; bounds and accuracy, the quietly examined skills
topics: Index laws with negative and fractional powers
week_7_8.practice: An indices, surds and bounds set including accuracy questions.

week_9_10.title: Expressions, Expansion and Factorisation
week_9_10.description: Expressions, expansion and factorisation with the area model underneath
week_9_10.practice: An expand-and-factorise set anchored by area-model diagrams.

week_11_12.title: Linear Equations, Inequalities and Formulae
week_11_12.description: Linear equations and inequalities read as sentences; changing the subject of formulae
topics: Solving linear equations step by step
Changing the subject of a formula
Reading an equation as a sentence
week_11_12.practice: Linear equation, inequality and rearrangement questions with worded contexts.

week_13_14.title: Straight-Line Graphs as One Story
week_13_14.description: Straight-line graphs: gradient as rate, intercepts as starting values, equation, table and graph as one story
topics: Equation, table and graph as one story
Finding the equation of a line
week_13_14.practice: Straight-line graph questions linking equation, table and graph.

week_15_16.title: Simultaneous Equations and Sequences
week_15_16.description: Simultaneous equations by structure; sequences and the nth term
topics: Finding and using the nth term rule
week_15_16.practice: A simultaneous-equations and nth-term set graded by structure.

month_5_6.title: Months 5-6: Shape, Space and Data Foundations

week_17_18.title: Angle Reasoning and Justification
week_17_18.description: Angle reasoning: parallel lines, polygons and circle basics with justification language
topics: Angles on lines and around points
Interior and exterior angles of polygons
Stating a reason for every step
week_17_18.practice: Angle problems requiring a named reason for each step.

week_19_20.title: Perimeter, Area, Volume and Pythagoras
week_19_20.description: Perimeter, area and volume including compound shapes; Pythagoras from a picture
topics: Perimeter and area of standard shapes
Volume and surface area of solids
Units for length, area and volume
week_19_20.practice: A mensuration set combining compound shapes and Pythagoras problems.

week_21_22.title: Statistics Foundations
week_21_22.description: Statistics foundations: averages, charts and scatter diagrams read critically
topics: Bar charts, pie charts and pictograms
week_21_22.practice: A statistics set interpreting charts and a scatter diagram.

week_23_24.title: Phase 1 Core-Tier Checkpoint
week_23_24.description: Phase checkpoint: a full Core-tier paper under timing, reviewed against the mark scheme, the review ritual for the rest of the course
topics: Error log started from the checkpoint
week_23_24.practice: A complete Core-tier paper sat to time, then reviewed against the mark scheme.
week_23_24.assessment: Core-tier checkpoint paper marked to the mark scheme, establishing the review ritual and the error log.

phase_2_extended.title: PHASE 2: The Extended Reach, With Settling Time (Months 7-12)
phase_2_extended.description: The topics that separate Extended from Core, and top grades from safe passes, taught months before the exam so they are owned, not crammed: functions, trigonometry beyond right angles, vectors, and probability at full depth.

week_25_26.title: Solving Quadratics Three Ways
week_25_26.description: Quadratic equations by factorising, completing the square and the formula, chosen by structure
week_25_26.practice: A quadratics set requiring choice of factorising, completing the square or the formula.

week_27_28.title: Quadratic Graphs and Linear-Quadratic Systems
week_27_28.description: Quadratic graphs and real contexts; simultaneous linear-quadratic systems
week_27_28.practice: Quadratic-graph questions plus simultaneous linear-quadratic systems.

week_29_30.title: Functions: Composite and Inverse
week_29_30.description: Function notation, composite and inverse functions, read as meaning, where half of all function marks are lost
topics: Inverse functions and how to find them
week_29_30.practice: A functions set focused on composite and inverse questions.

week_31_32.title: Graph Transformations and Graph Literacy
week_31_32.description: Graph transformations and the graph-literacy the top questions assume
topics: Graph literacy the top questions assume
week_31_32.practice: Graph-transformation and graph-reading questions at Extended standard.

month_9_10.title: Months 9-10: Trigonometry and Vectors at Full Depth

week_33_34.title: Trigonometry: Right Triangles to Sine and Cosine Rules
week_33_34.description: Right-triangle trig consolidated, then the sine and cosine rules with bearing and area applications
topics: Finding sides and angles in right triangles
Area of a triangle with the sine formula
week_33_34.practice: A trig set moving from right triangles to sine and cosine rule problems.

week_35_36.title: Trig Graphs and Equations
week_35_36.description: Trig graphs and solving simple trig equations (Extended reach)
topics: Features and periodicity of trig graphs
week_35_36.practice: Trig-graph and simple trig-equation questions at Extended reach.

week_37_38.title: Vectors and Geometric Proof
week_37_38.description: Vectors: notation, geometry proofs and the classic Extended vector question, defanged
week_37_38.practice: Vector questions building to the classic Extended vector proof.

week_39_40.title: Circle Theorems
week_39_40.description: Circle theorems completed with proof-quality justification
topics: Angle at the centre and at the circumference
week_39_40.practice: Circle-theorem problems requiring a named theorem for each step.

month_11_12.title: Months 11-12: Probability, Data and the Extended Checkpoint

week_41_42.title: Probability and Tree Diagrams
week_41_42.description: Probability with tree diagrams and conditional reasoning; combined events without formula-panic
topics: Probability of single and combined events
week_41_42.practice: A probability set using tree diagrams and conditional reasoning.

week_43_44.title: Cumulative Frequency and Histograms
week_43_44.description: Cumulative frequency, histograms and comparing distributions like a statistician
week_43_44.practice: A set drawing and reading cumulative frequency curves and histograms.

week_45_46.title: Introductory Differentiation (Extended)
week_45_46.description: Differentiation introduced (0580 Extended's calculus taste): gradients of curves and turning points
topics: Differentiation as the gradient of a curve
week_45_46.practice: Introductory differentiation questions finding gradients and turning points.

week_47_48.title: Phase 2 Extended-Tier Checkpoint
week_47_48.description: Phase checkpoint: full Extended-tier past paper cycle with mark-scheme review and error-log update
week_47_48.practice: A full Extended-tier past paper sat to time and reviewed against the mark scheme.
week_47_48.assessment: Extended-tier past-paper cycle marked to the mark scheme, updating the error log and weak-topic list.

phase_3_additional_and_exam.title: PHASE 3: Additional Maths 0606 + Exam Craft (Months 13-18)
phase_3_additional_and_exam.description: Strong students extend into Additional Mathematics, functions, identities, vectors and introductory calculus taught properly. All students then enter the exam machine: past-paper cycles by exact board and series, non-calculator discipline, and mark-scheme craft that peaks in the exam window.

week_49_52.title: 0606 Functions and Algebra Foundations
week_49_52.description: 0606: functions at depth, quadratics revisited with rigour, indices and surds, simultaneous equations, the A-Level trailer begins. (Parallel track: Extended students consolidate by error-log priority)
topics: Parallel Extended consolidation by error-log priority
week_49_52.practice: 0606 function and algebra problems, with Extended students on a parallel consolidation set.

week_53_56.title: 0606 Binomial, Trig Identities and Logs
week_53_56.description: 0606: binomial expansion, trigonometric identities and equations, logarithmic and exponential functions
week_53_56.practice: A 0606 set on binomial expansion, trig identities and log-exponential functions.

week_57_60.title: 0606 Calculus, Vectors and Kinematics
week_57_60.description: 0606: introductory calculus, differentiation and integration with applications; vectors and kinematics
topics: Integration as the reverse of differentiation
week_57_60.practice: A 0606 calculus set with differentiation, integration and a kinematics problem.

month_16_17.title: Months 16-17: Past-Paper Craft by Board and Series

week_61_64.title: Board-Exact Paper Cycles
week_61_64.description: Paper cycles from your exact board: non-calculator papers under bare-hands discipline, calculator papers with method-mark craft
topics: Paper cycles from the exact board and series
week_61_64.practice: Board-exact non-calculator and calculator papers sat to time and reviewed.

week_65_68.title: Timing Plans and Point-Leak Habits
week_65_68.description: Timing plans from personal data; multi-step question chains rehearsed; the plus-minus and units habits that stop point leaks
topics: Personal timing plans from paper data
week_65_68.practice: Timed multi-step question sets drilling the plus-minus and units habits.

month_18.title: Month 18: The Exam Series, Met Calmly

week_69_70.title: Final Cycles and Error-Log Closure
week_69_70.description: Final full cycles with documented score progression; error-log closure
week_69_70.practice: Final full paper cycles with progression charted and closed errors re-tested.

week_71_72.title: Peak-Week Plan and Exam-Day Routines
week_71_72.description: Peak-week plan: light consolidations, rest, and exam-day routines rehearsed, the series met as a known place
topics: Light consolidation in the peak week
The series met as a known place
week_71_72.practice: Light consolidation tasks and a rehearsed exam-day routine before the series.
week_71_72.assessment: Final full-cycle rehearsal with documented score progression before the exam series.

faqs.question: Which boards and syllabuses does this course cover?
faqs.answer: Cambridge IGCSE Mathematics 0580 and 0980 at both tiers, Cambridge Additional Mathematics 0606, and Edexcel International GCSE (4MA1). The first thing the mentor confirms is the exact code and tier your school uses, and all past-paper work comes from that board and series.

faqs.question: Core or Extended, how should we decide?
faqs.answer: By destination, then evidence. Core caps the achievable grade, so if A-Level maths, sciences or a competitive sixth form is even possible, Extended is the default. Whether the gaps blocking Extended are repairable in the time available is a diagnostic question, and the free demo answers it with evidence you can take to school.

faqs.question: What about the non-calculator papers?
faqs.answer: Cambridge's updated 0580/0980 syllabus put a non-calculator paper in front of every candidate at both tiers. We train that fluency deliberately, fractions, percentages and estimation drills open every class, and verify current paper structures against your school's syllabus code and series.

faqs.question: Should my child take Additional Maths 0606?
faqs.answer: If they are comfortable in Extended and A-Level maths is likely: strongly consider it. 0606 is the best A-Level preparation on the syllabus list, functions, identities, vectors and introductory calculus taught properly. It is a genuine second subject that rewards starting early, which is exactly how this course sequences it.

faqs.question: My child does endless past papers but the score is stuck. Will more classes help?
faqs.answer: More papers will not, practice repeats what the student already does, errors included. A stuck score almost always means an understanding gap upstream, usually algebra as memorized rules. We find the gap, fix it at the root, and then past papers start converting effort into marks again.

faqs.question: Can this course rescue a threatened Core entry?
faqs.answer: Often, yes. Schools respond to evidence, and entry deadlines usually leave more room than parents fear. A focused term of understanding-first teaching plus improving test scores is the argument that works, and we are honest in both directions when Core genuinely is the kind choice.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1, while IGCSE-specialist tutors bill $40 to $100 per hour for the same format. 8 live one-hour classes a month, recordings included, free demo first.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every student starts with a free live demo class that doubles as the diagnostic, board, tier, fluency and algebra base, with no card details required.

testimonials.name: Sonam Oswal, mother of Dhairya (verified Google review)
testimonials.text: My child Dhairya is really enjoying the classes. This is his first online class, and he eagerly looks forward to it. I can see his improvement.

course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
